inline_comments.message:
1. please keep the naming convention to define maven variable. Something like "mso.git.url", etc ...
2. Do not hard-code the value to https://gerrit.openecomp.org/r, please keep the ${env.GIT_NO_PROJECT}. This variable is defined on the LF environment for jenkins
When compiling locally, build is failing because it can't resolve ${env.GIT_NO_PROJECT}, so how should I proceed to fix local build if I can't hard-code the value in a maven property?

As is, you must set the env variable "GIT_NO_PROJECT" before starting the mvn command.

Now, I understand that you want to provide it as a maven param (with -Dmso.git.url=<GitRepoUrl> in argument).

Therefore what I suggest is:
1. Rename GERRIT_URL to mso.git.url (to stay in line with the naming convention)
2. The default value for this new variable must be set to "${env.GIT_NO_PROJECT}". Because this env var is defined in the jenkins jobs to do the release/staging builds.

> Now, I understand that you want to provide it as a maven param (with -Dmso.git.url=<GitRepoUrl> in argument).

No, this is not really what I'm saying. This would mean each time one wants to compile the mso/package/docker project, one will have to pass as argument the `mso.git.url`. This doesn't make sense as this url will/should never change, so it should be fine hard-coding it.
I'm still not sure to understand the benefit of this env.GIT_NO_PROJECT variable that Jenkins populate.

I understand this URL might change in the near future to reflect onap instead of ecomp, but this change shouldn't be the driver of not hard-coding a value that will be sealed in stone afterward.

Am I missing something?

> 1. Rename GERRIT_URL to mso.git.url (to stay in line with the naming convention)

Ack.

This would mean each time one wants to compile the mso/package/docker project, one will have to pass as argument the `mso.git.url`. This doesn't make sense as this url will/should never change, so it should be fine hard-coding it.

--> Yes and I understand very well, that's annoying :-(
But what you are missing is: 
You break our jenkins build with this(Docker profile enabled). We need to change the jenkins job template if we go there. Currently the var GIT_NO_PROJECT defines a SSH gerrit link with a specific user for jenkins.
Note that: 
- The jenkins job executed in the Pull Request DOES not execute the docker profile so your change can't be validated.
- Openecomp gerrit is not yet public and requires creds to access the code over HTTPS

So unless we change the jenkins templates for all ECOMP components, the default value must be set to ${env.GIT_NO_PROJECT}

We could probably add a comment in the pom description:
If the maven profile "docker" is specified the parameter 
-Dmso.git.url=<MsoGitRepo> must be provided !
e.g:
mvn clean install -P docker -Dmso.git.url=https://gerrit.openecomp.org/r
